body.title: test_coding_prompt_byte_identical red — coding prompt drifted from pinned SHA256
body.description:
PRE-EXISTING failure (unrelated to the router cutover; found during T-inference-migrate-consumers-cutover). tests/inference/test_domain_prompts.py::test_coding_prompt_byte_identical_to_prior_ds_prompt asserts sha256(coding domain prompt) == _CODING_PROMPT_SHA256; the prompt text has drifted from the pinned constant. Decide: re-pin the SHA (accept the intentionally-updated prompt) or revert the prompt drift. Verify the change was intended before re-pinning.

**Test plan:** tests/inference/test_domain_prompts.py::test_coding_prompt_byte_identical_to_prior_ds_prompt passes (SHA re-pinned to the intended prompt or prompt reverted).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: DUPLICATE of T-repin-coding-prompt-sha256 — same failing node (test_domain_prompts.py, the coding-prompt SHA pin), same cause (the pin went stale when 124553ee intentionally stripped six hardcoded ~/dev/src/UnseenUniversity paths). Resolved there: verified the drift was that commit alone, re-pinned, renamed the node to say what it guards, and added the discriminating no-absolute-paths property test. Commits 87720b30 -> e2eb667f. Two tickets for one red is itself the finding — the second was filed a day later without a duplicate check.
body.unproven_reason: Closed as a duplicate, so it carries the proof status of T-repin-coding-prompt-sha256 (shipped-unproven: a test-only hash re-pin has no implementation delta and thus no red form a hollow re-pin would fail).
